topics.title: Dynamic Brain States and Consciousness
topics.description: This cluster centers on dynamic patterns of functional connectivity in the human brain, especially how brain states vary across rest and task conditions. It also links these dynamics to consciousness, arousal, anesthesia, and individual differences such as brain fingerprinting.

topics.title: Neurodegenerative disease progression and biomarkers
topics.description: The cluster centers on major neurodegenerative diseases, especially Alzheimer disease and Parkinson disease, across preclinical to dementia stages. It highlights biomarker and structural changes linked to cognitive decline, amyloid and tau pathology, and disease progression in aging populations.

topics.title: Psychiatric Risk and Adversity in Youth
topics.description: This cluster centers on psychiatric disorders and symptom development across adolescence and youth, especially schizophrenia, psychosis, depression, and bipolar disorder. It emphasizes childhood adversity, early life stress, trauma, and resilience as transdiagnostic factors shaping vulnerability and psychopathology.

topics.title: Neural Representations in Visual Imagery and Action
topics.description: This cluster centers on how visual imagery, action observation, and agency are represented in the human brain, especially in visual and motor regions. It also includes decoding and fMRI studies that probe neural correlates, working memory, and related neural mechanisms.

topics.title: Deep Learning for Brain MRI Segmentation
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep learning approaches for automated brain MRI segmentation and related reconstruction workflows. It emphasizes model validation, robustness, and reproducibility across atlases and segmentation pipelines.

topics.title: Brain Development Across Childhood and Adolescence
topics.description: This cluster centers on normative and altered brain development from infancy through adolescence, with emphasis on developmental trajectories, brain structure, and white matter changes. It also includes sex differences, pubertal influences, and links to cognitive outcomes such as working memory and behavior.

topics.title: Neural Bases of Emotion and Social Processing
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain systems support emotion regulation, emotion processing, and social cognition, with emphasis on anxiety, fear, and related affective symptoms. It also includes work on amygdala function, oxytocin, interoception, and connectivity-based evidence from fMRI.

topics.title: Neuromodulation for Mood and Anxiety Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on neuromodulation approaches for psychiatric conditions, especially treatment resistant depression and obsessive compulsive disorder. It also includes studies of functional connectivity and neurophysiological measures linked to treatment response and efficacy.

topics.title: Network and Outcome Changes After Brain Injury
topics.description: Studies in this cluster examine how stroke, epilepsy, and traumatic brain injury alter brain networks, connectivity, and disconnection patterns. They also focus on localizing epileptogenic or seizure onset zones and predicting functional, motor, language, and cognitive outcomes.

topics.title: High-Resolution fMRI Signal and Motion Challenges
topics.description: The cluster centers on high-resolution fMRI at ultra-high field strengths, emphasizing BOLD sensitivity, vascular effects, and cerebrovascular reactivity. It also highlights practical challenges such as head motion, artifacts, and denoising strategies including multi echo approaches.

keywords: accurate voxel wise brain age prediction
topics.title: Brain Age and Cognitive Aging
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain age estimation in the aging human brain, including predictive modeling and age-related structural and functional changes. It also links brain age measures to cognition, cognitive performance, and cognitive impairment.

topics.title: Language Processing and Neural Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on the neural basis of language and speech, including auditory processing, comprehension, and lateralization. It also includes clinical and developmental topics such as aphasia, dyslexia, multilingual speakers, and developmental changes.

topics.title: Neurocircuitry of craving and addiction
topics.description: This cluster centers on addiction-related disorders and the neural mechanisms underlying craving, reward, and inhibitory control across substances such as alcohol, cannabis, opioids, and methamphetamine. It also includes intervention- and challenge-related studies involving psilocybin and ketamine.

topics.title: Developmental autism subgroups and brain variation
topics.description: This cluster centers on autism and related developmental conditions across childhood and adolescence, emphasizing symptom profiles, subgroup differences, and clinical variation. The abstracts link behavioral characteristics with brain abnormalities and developmental changes in autistic individuals.

topics.title: Open Neuroimaging Infrastructure and Workflows
topics.description: This cluster centers on open, collaborative neuroimaging infrastructure for data organization, processing, visualization, and workflow execution. It emphasizes standards-based tools and platforms that support reproducible and clinically actionable analysis across shared datasets.

keywords: functional connectivity pathways support fear avoidance processes
topics.title: Brain Mechanisms in Chronic Pain Syndromes
topics.description: This cluster focuses on chronic and widespread pain conditions, including fibromyalgia, chronic migraine, trigeminal neuralgia, and juvenile fibromyalgia. It emphasizes pain perception, central sensitization, fear-avoidance processes, and brain-based markers and connectivity mechanisms underlying pain experience.

keywords: localised high amplitude slow local sleep wave
topics.title: Human Sleep Dynamics and Neural States
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the neural dynamics and physiology of human sleep, especially NREM sleep, arousal transitions, and stage-specific brain activity. It also highlights sleep microstructure markers such as K complexes, spindles, and local slow waves, alongside circuit-level features like locus coeruleus activity.
